Paragraph

When \(\bff :D\subset \bbR^{n}\mapsto \bbR^{m}\) is differentiable in \(D\text{,}\) then its Jacobian matrix \([D\bff (\bx)]\) can be considered as a map from \(D\) into vector space of \(m\times n\) matrices, which can be identified with \(\bbR^{m\times n}\text{.}\) Thus it makes sense to consider whether \([D\bff (\bx)]\) is differentiable in \(D\text{.}\)
in-context
